sections.heading: Syntax
sections.body: Propositional logic's syntax defines how to build valid sentences from propositional symbols (like P, Q, standing for statements that are either true or false) combined using logical connectives — for example, "P AND Q" is a well-formed sentence.

sections.heading: Semantics
sections.body: Semantics defines what a sentence actually means — specifically, the rules for determining whether a sentence is true or false given an assignment of truth values to its propositional symbols.

sections.heading: Logical Connectives
sections.body: The standard connectives are AND (∧, conjunction), OR (∨, disjunction), NOT (¬, negation), IMPLIES (→, conditional), and IF AND ONLY IF (↔, biconditional) — these let simple propositions be combined into more complex statements.

sections.heading: Truth Tables
sections.body: A truth table exhaustively lists every possible combination of true/false values for a sentence's propositional symbols, alongside the resulting truth value of the whole sentence — a mechanical way to verify what a logical sentence means in every possible case.

sections.heading: Inference
sections.body: Inference is the process of deriving new true sentences from existing ones using valid logical rules — for example, from "P → Q" and "P" being true, we can infer "Q" is true (a rule known as modus ponens).

sections.heading: Resolution
sections.body: Resolution is a single, powerful inference rule that works on sentences in a specific normal form (clauses) — it's the basis of many automated theorem-proving systems, since it can be applied mechanically and repeatedly to derive new conclusions or prove a contradiction.

quiz.question: A truth table is used to:
options: Exhaustively show a sentence's truth value for every combination of its symbols' truth values
Replace the need for logical connectives

quiz.question: Resolution is best described as:
options: A single inference rule, on clause-form sentences, underlying many automated theorem provers
A way to acquire knowledge from experts
